title: Suction Pressure and Airflow in Vacuum Systems
content: Vacuum cleaner performance is characterised by sealed suction (maximum pressure difference at zero flow, Pa) and airflow (maximum flow at zero pressure, m³/s), and is traded off along the fan's performance curve.
extended_content: Bernoulli's principle and the fan affinity laws govern the suction-airflow curve.  Airflow (volumetric) Q and pressure drop ΔP relate to particle pickup: heavy particles (sand, crumbs) require high ΔP; light particles (dust, hair) require high Q. Robot vacuums: typical sealed suction 1000–4000 Pa, airflow 5–20 L/s.  Suction power P_air = ΔP × Q (watts).  HEPA filter reduces Q by 15–30% at the same motor speed; system losses include cyclone separator, duct bends, and brush roll.  Fan affinity: Q ∝ N, ΔP ∝ N², P ∝ N³ where N = rotational speed.
formula: P_air = ΔP · Q ;  Q ∝ N,  ΔP ∝ N²,  P ∝ N³
units: ΔP: Pa, Q: m³/s (or L/s), P_air: W
source: Çengel & Cimbala, Fluid Mechanics, 3rd ed.; IEC 62885 Vacuum Cleaner Test Standards
